kinderhook.name: Martin Van Buren Nature Trails
kinderhook.description: The 70-acres of Van Buren Nature Trails are made up of four well marked all season trails, including a 1.4-mile outer loop trail. A mowed meadow with two outhouses is used for group camping. There is also a glen with a small stream and overlook benches.

kinderhook.name: Samascott’s Garden Market
kinderhook.description: Growers of annuals, perennials and hanging baskets. Open all year round selling locally sourced fruits, vegetables, pumpkins, sweet corn, fresh eggs, free range beef, chicken and pork. Bakery on-site serves fresh bread, homemade soups, cider donuts, pies and seasonally prepared foods. Homemade hard ice cream all year and soft ice cream seasonally. Gift items, local honey, maple syrup, pasta, nuts and coffee. Corn maze every fall.

kinderhook.name: Drive Through History
kinderhook.description: Get out and explore the historic beauty of Columbia County with our self-guided road trips.

kinderhook.name: Columbia County Historical Society Museum & Library
James Vanderpoel House c. 1820
kinderhook.description: Also known as the “House of History”; originally the Federal-era home of prominent lawyer and politician James Vanderpoel and his family, the house reflects an elegant lifestyle in a prosperous, early 19th century upstate New York village.

kinderhook.name: Ichabod Crane Schoolhouse c.1850
kinderhook.description: The Schoolhouse, along with the Luykas Van Alen House (same property) is currently self-guided.

kinderhook.name: The Aviary Kinderhook
kinderhook.description: Eclectic rotating dinner menu highlighting produce from our local Hudson Valley farm partners. Housed under the soaring cathedral ceiling of the historic Kinderhook Knitting Mill.

kinderhook.name: Luykas Van Alen House c. 1737
kinderhook.description: Designated a National Historic Landmark in 1967, the Luykas Van Alen house is a restored house museum representing 18th century rural Dutch farm life in the Hudson River Valley.

kinderhook.name: Rothermel Park & Playground
kinderhook.description: A popular community-gathering space that contains three baseball fields, a tennis court, a pavilion, a playground, a public bathroom and an Albany-Hudson Electric Trail Trailhead with parking.

kinderhook.name: Persons of Color Cemetery
kinderhook.description: In 1815 Kinderhook landowner John Rogers stipulated that a portion of his property be “intended as a cemetery for the people of color.” Although only fifteen headstones remain, it is estimated that hundreds of individuals may have been interred here.

kinderhook.name: Super Stories
kinderhook.description: A Makers Space that focuses on community storytelling through public art, hands-on making experiences, and partnerships with local artists and educators.

kinderhook.name: Mills Park
kinderhook.description: A one-acre managed ecological park located approximately .2 miles from the historic center of the Village of Kinderhook.

kinderhook.name: OK Pantry
kinderhook.description: A modern-designed space with warm service, handcrafted beverages, REALLY good coffee, fresh pastries, lifestyle goods, grocery beyond.

kinderhook.name: Isola Wine & Tapas
kinderhook.description: A downtown Kinderhook oasis that specializes in wine and tapas from European islands and coasts.

kinderhook.name: Concerts in the Village
kinderhook.description: Exceptional Classical Music Series performed in Kinderhook, New York. Choral and Orchestra directed by David Smith.

kinderhook.name: The School: Jack Shainman Gallery
kinderhook.description: The focus of the gallery is to exhibit, represent and champion artists from around the world, in particular artists from Africa, East Asia, and North America, by mounting major exhibitions of their work in the gallery. Open Sat 11am-6pm and by appointment.

kinderhook.name: Martin Van Buren National Historic Site
kinderhook.description: The home and farm of the founder of the Democratic Party and the eighth president of the United States.

kinderhook.name: The Brooklyn Pizzeria
kinderhook.description: Taste the Difference right here! Brooklyn Pizza made by a Brooklyn Guy. Pizza is a way of life for our family, We have been making pizza for 75 years. We only use all the best ingredients in all of our pizzas!

kinderhook.name: Samascott Orchards
kinderhook.description: Open June-October for pick-your-own fruits and vegetables on 200 acres. Apple cider mill on premises. Farm stand offers apple cider, donuts, homemade hard ice cream, fresh eggs, free-range beef, pork and chicken. Open Daily 8am-6pm, in season

kinderhook.name: Morningbird
kinderhook.description: All-day coffeehouse/cafe serving house-made pastries and Southeast Asian lunch dishes.

kinderhook.name: Saisonnier
kinderhook.description: Saisonnier is a bar shop specializing in independent craft beer and local cheeses and provisions. We carry 125 beers available to stay or to go as well as wine & cider and a thoughtfully crafted menu consisting of artisanal sandwiches, cheese and charcuterie boards. Enjoy our offerings in an environment with historic charm dating back to the 1830s, nestled in the quaint village of Kinderhook.

kinderhook.name: Broad Street Bagel Co.
kinderhook.description: Baked goods, bagels, sandwiches, wraps, soups, handcrafted coffee, and tea.

kinderhook.name: Kinderhook Farmers’ Market
kinderhook.description: Local farmers’ market offering a wide variety of goods: produce, naturally raised meats, free-range eggs, baked goods, flowers, bedding plants, honey, maple syrup, jams, draft beers, and crafts. Family activity areas and live music.

kinderhook.name: Martin Van Buren Grave Site
kinderhook.description: Burial place of Martin Van Buren, 8th President of the United States.

kinderhook.name: Del’s Roadside
kinderhook.description: Bringing together our community with farm-fresh handmade ice cream and delicious, local food, along with our NEW Upper Depot tap room. Hudson-based Upper Depot Brewing brings award-winning beers to Kinderhook. Enjoy curated cocktails and a rotating wine list.

kinderhook.name: September Gallery
kinderhook.description: SEPTEMBER is an art gallery, an open space, and an on-going practice. We exhibit and support contemporary artists who are committed to their work for life. We believe that being receptive to change is essential to the creative process. We provide a platform for exceptional individuals who have not been historically centered with care because of their inherited, chosen or perceived identities. We engage our local community while exhibiting artists from other cities and countries. We concentrate on visual artists, and collaborate with curators, performers, activists, poets, chefs, writers, and other creatives.

kinderhook.name: Old Dutch Inn
kinderhook.description: Situated in the heart of the historic Dutch Village of Kinderhook, Old Dutch Inn is a luxury, boutique hotel designed to service a contemporary clientele in a historic setting. The inn’s 9 guestrooms have all been appointed with elegant finishes, en suite marble bathrooms, and modern amenities. Named Outstanding Small Property of the Year by the New York State Hospitality and Tourism Association.

kinderhook.name: Kinderhook Books
kinderhook.description: In the Village of Kinderhook! Swing by for a good book or a bookish gift …….or come on over simply to share with us your favorite reads!

kinderhook.name: Hamrah’s Lebanese Foods
kinderhook.description: At Hamrah’s Lebanese Foods and Takeaway, we focus on bringing the delightful flavors of Lebanese cooking to your table. We offer a lunch menu that features pita wraps, rice bowls, and fresh salads, all made with uncomplicated and wholesome ingredients including local meats, fresh produce, and the highest quality Middle Eastern specialty seasonings.

businesses.name: Businesses
businesses.description: Cafe Mutton has opened a sibling restaurant in the space called Merkin — because of course they did — which chef-owner Shaina Loew-Banayan described as “an awesome new place to hang out, drink wine, listen to records, buy charcuterie, and eat sandwiches and maybe some soup.

businesses.name: Rocky Fresh Farm
businesses.description: Comprehensive collection of herbs sold as potted plants, dried, decorative wreaths, herb salts, and other herbal products.

businesses.name: Brook N Wood Family Campground
businesses.description: Nothing brings friends and families closer together than experiencing the great outdoors. We have 62 gorgeous acres! Everyone loves taking a stroll on our trail along the beautiful stream in the forest and taking in all nature has to offer in this peaceful environment. A refreshing dip in our modern, heated swimming pool can be enjoyed mid-May to mid-September. Our enclosed pavilion has billiards, a jukebox, arcade games, plenty of room for activity, crafts, and hosts our weekly Ice Cream Social.

businesses.name: Cooper & Company
businesses.description: A lovely selection of antiques, art, lamps, and custom, lampshades.

businesses.name: Gatherwild Ranch
businesses.description: Spread out on 15 acres of rolling hills in the Hudson Valley, Gatherwild provides unique shelters with simple yet luxurious interiors, where we encourage you to sit back and stay for a while. We embrace the slow life here, inspired by grassy picnics, evening bonfires, and star gazing.

businesses.name: Tiny Hearts Farm
businesses.description: Atypical flower shop/glorified farm stand. Haven to lovers of flowers, wild, and leaves. Flowers grown @tinyheartsfarm.

businesses.name: Churchtown Dairy
businesses.description: Churchtown Dairy is a working bio-dynamic farm that intends to serve as a beacon for those working to promote regenerative agriculture and sustainable communities. We support the development of resilient and sustainable food systems while raising public awareness of the positive impacts ecological agriculture can have on the health of the human being and the earth. We strive to imbue all our work with beauty—the buildings, the gardens, the care for the animals, and the economic and social systems within which we work. *We hope you will visit & stroll through our gardens, enjoy our shop, & say hello to our farmers, gardeners, cheesemakers and animals.

businesses.name: Nautical Nest Hudson
businesses.description: One of the newest boutique hotels in Hudson, ideally located in the center of downtown just a few steps from Warren St. We offer four beautiful private suites, all with en suite bathrooms, luxurious bedding and plenty of amenities to ensure a comfortable stay.

businesses.name: Grimaldi Farm Store
businesses.description: Farm store selling certified organic grass-fed beef.

businesses.name: Robin’s Nest
businesses.description: Browse our workshop featuring re-imagined furniture, antiques, designer clothing and jewelry. Maybe you have a family heirloom that has seen better days, you love it for its history but it doesn’t fit with your living style today. Let us work with you to reimagine how it can be updated to fit into your today lifestyle. Visit our shop to see works in progress and imagine how your treasure can look with a facelift.

businesses.name: Kea Carpets and Kilims
businesses.description: Kea ceaselessly shops the world to find unique and authentic vintage and antique tribal kilims and rugs. Our carefully curated collection is both diverse and eclectic, ranging from the long goat haired Tulus of Central Anatolia to the unusual Caucasian, Persian and Central Asian pieces. We share our long history of knowledge in the world of rugs, textile and design with you, every tribal piece is authenticated, dated and identified either by tribe or country of origin.

businesses.name: Flying Monkey Totes
businesses.description: One-of-kind, hand painted bags and accessories. Vegan & leather products available. Jewelry, Accessories, Apothecary, Clothing & more… custom orders welcome.

businesses.name: Les Indiennes
businesses.description: Hand block-printed organic cotton textiles and soft home furnishings made in India with all natural dyes.

businesses.name: Highland Farm
businesses.description: Your local source for responsibly farm-raised venison products and other game meats (venison, elk, bison, a wide variety of sausages, ostrich eggs and MORE!). We supply quality fresh and smoked products at reasonable prices to individuals, grocers and restaurants throughout the Hudson Valley and around the country.

businesses.name: Hover Farms
businesses.description: Hover Farms is a sixth generation family farm, focusing on sustainable farming methods. We strive to produce natural, grass-fed, high quality meats for our community. We raise Red Devon cattle and Katahdin sheep on two farms, using rotational grazing methods. We also produce pastured poultry, all natural eggs, and raw honey. Come visit our farm store, where you can find all of our home grown offerings along with a variety of local products from farmers and makers in our community.

businesses.name: Cold Spring Tree Farm
businesses.description: Seasonal Holiday/Christmas Trees and Greens Shop – Family owned and run for over 40 years!

businesses.name: High Falls Pizzeria & Tap House
businesses.description: A blend of classic Italian dining with American comfort food. Family friendly and locally owned.

businesses.name: Martin Van Buren Nature Trails

businesses.name: Davis Studio
businesses.description: Davis Studio was founded in 1998 by artist Mary Anne Davis. Moving to the Hudson Valley from New York City in 2000, Davis found life in the country a profound revelation. Inspired by the rural condition, the studio works carefully within the community and environment to contribute to a local, sustainable economy contributing to our creative territory by producing useful and accessible art.

Governor’s Tavern

businesses.description: The Mercantile, situated next to the barroom and restaurant, works double duty as a coffee shop by day and rum room by night.

Basilica Hudson
businesses.description: A reclaimed 1880s industrial factory, this multidisciplinary arts center supports the creation, production and presentation of arts and culture, and fosters the sustainable community. With events in music, performance, film, food and literary programming, as well as a regular film screening series and art exhibitions, Basilica Hudson invites the discovery of Hudson and the area.

businesses.name: Hudson Hall
businesses.description: Hudson Hall is a cultural beacon in the Hudson Valley, offering a dynamic year-round schedule of music, theater, dance, literature, workshops for youth and adults, as well as family programs and large-scale community events such as Winter Walk. Located in an historic landmark that houses New York State’s oldest surviving theater, Hudson Hall reopened following a yearlong historic restoration in April 2017. The newly restored Hudson Hall reflects Hudson’s rich history in a modern facility that welcomes residents and visitors from throughout our local community, across the nation, and around the globe.

businesses.name: Tasty Treats Trail
businesses.description: While you’re driving around beautiful Columbia County and your sweet tooth gets a hankering – what do you do?

businesses.name: Historic Hikes
businesses.description: Explore the rich history of Columbia County while experiencing all the outdoor enjoyment our historic properties have to offer. So put on your walking shoes, pack a snack and step back in time.

businesses.name: Empire State Trail
businesses.description: The 750-mile Empire State Trail showcases New York’s special places, diverse history, and iconic landscapes.

businesses.name: Harlem Valley Rail Trail
businesses.description: Welcome to the Harlem Valley Rail Trail, two sections totaling 26 paved miles through stunning Columbia and Dutchess County landscapes. Since the mid-1980’s, the Harlem Valley Rail Trail Association has dreamed of a 46-mile rail trail in the Harlem Valley and Taconic Hills of eastern New York. The first segment of the trail opened in 1996.

businesses.name: Overmountain Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: This location offers spectacular views of the Harlem Valley, Hudson Valley, and the Taconic Mountains.

businesses.name: Big Onion Walking Tours
businesses.description: A two-hour walk exploring the history, architecture, and people of the City of Hudson. Offered as a public walk as well as for private bookings.

businesses.name: Bicycling Columbia County
businesses.description: Columbia County’s unsurpassed rural beauty makes cycling through pastoral countryside, historic villages, and scenic vistas unique to this area. The self guided loops illustrated on this website break the territory into pleasurable, sometimes challenging rides of various lengths and ability levels. Every effort has been made to create rides that are safe however some segments may place riders on roads with fast moving traffic.

businesses.name: Scenic Sips of Columbia County
businesses.description: Explore the many purveyors of craft beverages around Columbia County while drinking in the abundant scenic views of our countryside. As you meander through our picturesque back roads, enjoy a restaurant, unique shop, museum, or historic site.

businesses.name: Chatham Real Food Market Co-op Café
businesses.description: A 100% Community owned grocery cooperative open to everyone. Carrying products from over 140 local and regional producers ranging from produce, meat, dairy, baked goods, grocery, health and wellness and more. The Cafe is open daily with fresh prepared hot bar and salad bar items.

businesses.name: Georgia Ray’s Kitchen
businesses.description: Cafe to go, as well as catering. Our Chef Danyell is a CIA graduate who has studied and worked in France, California and NYC for 10 years before coming home to op en up our commercial kitchen.

businesses.name: Capital Craft Beverage Trail
businesses.description: The Capital Craft Beverage Trail was formed in 2015 to promote craft and farm-based beverage producers in the Capital Region of New York State. This one-of-a-kind “drinking tour” promotes some of the region’s most popular craft beverage facilities. Every producer sources ingredients locally, and beverages are created in small batches to maximize quality.

businesses.name: Drive Through History
businesses.description: Get out and explore the historic beauty of Columbia County with our self-guided road trips.

businesses.name: Bartlett House
businesses.description: This restored historic property has been transformed into an active kitchen, bakery, and café serving specialty food items inspired by the Hudson Valley and representing a crossroads of fresh flavors and delights. Grab a coffee and pastry to go, or sit down and enjoy a full meal made by us.

businesses.name: Shady Acres Farm Market
businesses.description: We think knowing where your food comes from is an important part of shopping for your families’ groceries. That’s why we are so proud to announce the addition of our new farm market (Summer 2022)! With much more space and a dedicated staff to help you out, it’s never been easier to shop for farm fresh produce and locally raised meats from here in Canaan, NY.

businesses.name: Carrie Haddad Gallery
businesses.description: Established in 1991 as the first fine art gallery in Hudson, NY, Carrie Haddad Gallery represents professionally committed artists as well as emerging talent specializing in all types of painting, large and small sculpture, works on paper, and a variety of techniques in photography. We host 7 annual exhibits on the main floor of our 3000 sq ft. gallery space located right on Hudson’s Warren Street.

businesses.name: 510 Warren Street Gallery
businesses.description: We are an artist-owned gallery in Hudson since 2010, featuring contemporary, local artists.

businesses.name: Gulden Farm
businesses.description: We raise healthy, grass fed, grass finished beef. Our farm strives to employ only biodynamic regenerative farming techniques on 700 acres in the Hudson Valley.

businesses.name: Nolita’s Cafe
businesses.description: Specializing in fresh baked goods, breakfast wraps, soup, delicious specialty sandwiches, and burgers.

businesses.name: The Ruins at Sassafras
businesses.description: Home of the former Shaker Second Family settlement. The Second family ran a successful seed business in the Brethen’s Workshop (now The Stone House) and constructed chairs, cloaks, quilts and bonnets in the Sister’s Workshop. When fire destroyed the Sister’s Workshop in 1923, construction of Shaker Chairs moved to a storage building. That building is believed to be the last standing Shake Chair Factory in America. When you visit, you can travel by foot or golf cart to see what is left of the settlement, the graveyard, and artifacts & enjoy a glimpse into Shaker daily life.

businesses.name: The Ruins at Sassafras
businesses.description: We offer three unique options to stay on the property of the former Shaker Second Family settlement. Historic grounds to wander and experience Shaker ingenuity.

businesses.name: Lasting Joy Brewery
businesses.description: There is no place on earth to brew and enjoy beer than New York’s Hudson Valley. Home to a rich agricultural heritage, the Hudson Valley has led the way in localizing craft beer production from farm to table, expanding partnerships between farms and breweries to inspire beers grown, brewed and enjoyed right here at home. When you visit Lasting Joy, you will wind through Hudson Valley’s scenic rolling farmland on your way to our own agriculturally situated, nestled between rotating crop fields on a bucolic farm road.

businesses.name: Private Public Gallery
businesses.description: Director Christopher Freeman has developed Private Public in the heart of Hudson, NY, as a center for artists and curators to show large-scale work and as a space to bring internationally significant art to the burgeoning community.

businesses.name: Balloon NY
businesses.description: Have you always dreamed of taking a hot air balloon ride – floating peacefully across the landscape? If so, let us make your dream come true!

businesses.name: Johnston Transportation
businesses.description: Public transportation in Columbia County as well as private rentals available.

businesses.name: Chatham Provisions
businesses.description: We create simple and creative meal solutions for your busy lifestyle by using the freshest and finest ingredients our local farms and purveyors have to offer. We also offer tasty beverages and a fine craft beer and cider selection,

businesses.name: Taconic State Park
businesses.description: Camping (tent, trailer, cottages and cabins), swimming, fishing, extensive trail system offering biking and nature trails, cross country skiing and snowmobile trails, Bash Bish Falls (beautiful waterfall), deer hunting in season. Camping season: May – November; park open year-round, fee to enter Memorial Day – Labor Day.

businesses.name: Hudson Ferry Co.
businesses.description: We are a cross-river ferry that connects communities in the Hudson, NY area. We offer regularly scheduled ferry services from Hudson to Athens, thematic sightseeing tours, and private charters.

businesses.name: Town of Ghent Recreation Area
businesses.description: The Town of Ghent Recreation Area Tennis Courts are one of the most popular places to play Pickleball! There are 2 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are permanent but you will need to bring your own net. The courts are free to use.

businesses.name: Stair Galleries
businesses.description: Stair Galleries is one of the leading regional auction houses in America, primarily servicing the New York City metro area and tri-state region. Our main focus is fine and decorative arts from the 17th to 20th centuries.

businesses.name: Roots Holistic Wellness
businesses.description: We are a Community Wellness Center that recognizes that one size does not fit all. We have a variety of weekly classes for physical and mental well being……as well as monthly events. We provide services to help you with many ailments such as anxiety, inflammation, physical pain, etc. We have a small shop at the front of space dedicated to locally made wellness products that go along with the services that we provide.

businesses.name: Roe Jan Brewing Co.
businesses.description: Roe Jan Brewing Company is a craft brewery and restaurant located in historic Hillsdale, NY. We produce a rotating lineup of well-balanced beers, ranging from traditional ales and lagers to trending styles like IPAs and sours. Our inventive pub menu features wood-fired food made 100% from scratch, right down to the hamburger buns. Our spacious dining room includes a large octagonal bar surrounding a vintage grain hopper and a variety of seating options including café tables, large communal tables and cozy club chairs. Dog-friendly outdoor seating is available on our patio and wraparound deck.

businesses.name: Hudson High School
businesses.description: Open gym time for those interested in playing the popular game of Pickleball. Pickleball is a racquet sport that combines elements of badminton, tennis, and table tennis. Two, three, or four players use solid paddles made of wood or composite materials to hit a ball, similar to a whiffle ball, over a net. Instruction will be provided the first couple of weeks from a local Pickleball player. It provides plenty of fun and exercise for men, women, young and old alike! Some paddles & balls are available – players are encouraged to bring their own.

businesses.description: New!!! We now have 4 Pickleball courts open for play!

businesses.name: Austerlitz History Center
businesses.description: The Center portrays the history of the Austerlitz area from its settlement in the 1750’s to 2021, through a series of wall panels and exhibits. Highlights include the Spencers Town Proprietors Book (1757-72), 1830’s portraits on wood of Sherman and Lydia Griswold, restored chemical fire engine purchased for Spencertown in 1915, “Austerlitz Archeology” (coins and other artifacts from the 1700’s unearthed by metal detector Max Cane), and special exhibit on the history of public education in Austerlitz and New York State.

businesses.name: Copake Iron Works Historic Site
businesses.description: One of the most intact examples of 19th century industrial iron-making in the northeast, featuring the centerpiece Blast Furnace, a visitor museum, and many other original buildings and structures. In 2023, the Pomeroy Family Railroad began running at the Copake Iron Works Historic site. The train and its route replicate the narrow gauge railroad that circumnavigated the site in the 19th century during the ironworks’ operating heyday. The train’s engine pulls two ore carts that have been retrofitted to carry passengers of all ages and abilities. The Pomeroy Family Railroad runs Saturdays, Sundays and holidays from Memorial Day to mid-October. Rides are free and no reservations are required.

businesses.name: Olana State Historic Site
businesses.description: Olana is the greatest masterwork of Frederic Edwin Church (1826-1900), the most famous American landscape artist of the mid-19th century the most important artist’s home, studio and designed landscape in the United States. Church designed Olana as a holistic environmental conservation. Olana’s 250-acre artist-designed landscape with five miles of carriage roads and a Persian-inspired house at its summit embraces unrivaled panoramic views of the Hudson Valley and Catskill mountains and welcomes more than 170,000 visitors annually. The landscape and house are open for touring, and reservations are highly recommended. The landscape is open daily 81m – sunset.

businesses.name: Shaker Museum
businesses.description: Shaker Museum is building a 21st-century museum in downtown Chatham, NY, to showcase the world’s most comprehensive collection of Shaker material culture and archives. In addition, Shaker Museum stewards Mount Lebanon Shaker Village, whose grounds are free and open to the public year-round.

businesses.name: FASNY Museum of Firefighting
businesses.description: With 70 vintage and rare fire engines dating back to 1731, on exhibit, whether you’re a firefighter, a student of history, or a family looking for a fun and educational day trip, you will not want to miss America’s interactive museum of firefighting. Activities for families include creative play on selected fire engines, many hands-on exhibits and a discovery room.

businesses.name: Hudson River Skywalk
businesses.description: The Hudson River Skywalk is a historic and scenic walkway that connects the home and studio of the major Hudson River School artists Thomas Cole and Frederic Church, over the Hudson River and across the Rip Van Winkle Bridge. The walk offers sweeping views of the Hudson River Valley and the Catskill Mountains.

businesses.name: OK Spoke Bicycle Shop
businesses.description: Bike shop with bicycle repair services. We also offer a full selection of bicycles and accessories. Bike rentals available.

businesses.name: The Aviary Kinderhook
businesses.description: Eclectic rotating dinner menu highlighting produce from our local Hudson Valley farm partners. Housed under the soaring cathedral ceiling of the historic Kinderhook Knitting Mill.

businesses.name: Honey Dog Farm Stand
businesses.description: Certified organic & eco-friendly, Honey Dog Farm is dedicated to sustainable, local agriculture. The Farm Stand, located at Long Hill Farm, is open June-December offering sunflowers, garlic, and a bounty of delicious farm-fresh vegetables – including beans, peas, carrots, celery, cucumbers, eggplant, leeks, and more!

businesses.name: Historians of Columbia County
businesses.description: This website is an educational website dedicated to engaging diverse audiences in the study of appreciation of the collective heritage of Columbia County, NY, and its environs. Its primary purpose is to promote public interest and participation in the preservation of the rich cultural heritage of our beautiful county.

businesses.name: Woodlife Farm Market
businesses.description: Woodlife Farm Market is a destination for anyone that appreciates a spectacular array of locally cultivated delights. Our farm stand offers maple syrup and honey made on-site as well as local products including pickles, sauces, dressings, jams, and bread mixes. Pickled veggies including beets and asparagus as well as fresh local produce, meats, and cheeses will also be stocked through summer.

businesses.name: Farm Shoppe
businesses.description: Offering farm-grown seasonal produce and florals, farm-made provisions and select pantry items, along with a highly curates assortment of both whimsical and elegant antique table attire. From unique and functional gifts to chef-chosen kitchen tools, Farm Shoppe has something for everyone who enjoys food, farming and fun!

businesses.name: Padrona
businesses.description: Fast casual cocktail bar with a focus on hospitality, fun and good drinks!

businesses.name: Hudson Sandwich Shop
businesses.description: Open for delivery and pick up – freshly made sandwiches for every craving!

businesses.name: Luykas Van Alen House c. 1737
businesses.description: Designated a National Historic Landmark in 1967, the Luykas Van Alen house is a restored house museum representing 18th century rural Dutch farm life in the Hudson River Valley.

businesses.name: Vosburgh Brewing
businesses.description: Come out and enjoy a NYS craft beer or cider in our early 1800s Dutch post and beam barn, or head outside and grab a seat amongst our flower and hop fields. The brewery resides on a working apple farm.

businesses.name: Little Apple Cidery
businesses.description: An organic orchard certified by NOFA-NY and specializes in heirloom and classic Northeast apple varieties. Fresh cider is produced in season (September-November). Hard ciders available year round. Tours and custom pressings offered – please call ahead.

businesses.name: Neverstill Wines Tasting Room
businesses.description: The gorgeous new tasting room pays homage to the beauty of the vineyard near Seneca Lake…..the soil, the climate, and the particulars that fall into winemaking.

businesses.name: Via Cassia
businesses.description: Via Cassia is casual trattoria style restaurant just like you would find down any side street in any Italian city, something simple and easy, with a rustic menu, and interesting wine. Owned and operated by Chef Gaetano Arnone, his career started with 10 years in NYC at Babbo Ristorante, followed by 4 years in Tuscany, and eventually settling into Hudson, opening Via Cassia with his wife Meigan Arnone in March 2024.

businesses.name: Disc Golf
businesses.description: Lake Taghkanic State Park now has Disc Golf!

businesses.name: Fork & Whisk
businesses.description: In the heart of the quaint village of Chatham, come visit this wonderful cafe/bakery with delightful eats from Chef Dana.

businesses.name: Chatham Skate Park
businesses.description: 2,500 sq. ft. concrete skate park free and open to the public, dawn ’til dusk, weather permitting.

businesses.name: Rothermel Park & Playground
businesses.description: A popular community-gathering space that contains three baseball fields, a tennis court, a pavilion, a playground, a public bathroom and an Albany-Hudson Electric Trail Trailhead with parking.

businesses.name: Hudson Athens Lighthouse
businesses.description: The northernmost lighthouse on the Hudson River rises from the river like a tiny gem. Visitors may view the lighthouse from the Henry Hudson Riverfront Park. Tours are offered the fourth Saturday from July through October and by special arrangement.

businesses.name: Persons of Color Cemetery
businesses.description: In 1815 Kinderhook landowner John Rogers stipulated that a portion of his property be “intended as a cemetery for the people of color.” Although only fifteen headstones remain, it is estimated that hundreds of individuals may have been interred here.

businesses.name: Lake Taghkanic State Park
businesses.description: Camping (tent, trailer, cottages and cabins), swimming, boat rentals, kayak rentals and kayaking, trails, ice fishing, skating, cross country skiing, disc golf, and snowmobile trails; deer and turkey hunting (bow only) in designated areas in season. Camping season: May-October; park open year-round, fee to enter Memorial Day – Labor Day.

businesses.name: Art School of Columbia County
businesses.description: The Art School of Columbia County (ASCC) provides free and affordable fine art programming serving around 1800 members of the community each year.

businesses.name: The Ghent Playhouse
businesses.description: Voted the Best Community Theater in Eastern NY -NY Theater Guide, “the Ghent” presents five diverse productions each season from October-June with talent from all around the area. It is also the home of the PantoLoons’ annual Thanksgiving Panto, a delightful British holiday tradition. Come experience the magic in this “gem” of a theater.

businesses.name: Crellin Community Park
businesses.description: Beautiful public park………one of the Town of Chatham’s two public parks that is walking distance from the Village of Chatham. Enjoy the swimming pond and beach, playgrounds, tennis courts, concrete skate park, soccer fields, baseball fields, basketball courts, community garden, Children’s Garden, skate park, pump tracks, two pavilions (rentable), snack bar and fitness/nature trail!

businesses.name: Super Stories
businesses.description: A Makers Space that focuses on community storytelling through public art, hands-on making experiences, and partnerships with local artists and educators.

businesses.name: Spencertown Inn
businesses.description: The Spencertown Inn is an all-suite luxury Inn. With just 5-suites in total, the Inn offers quiet and private accommodations in a country setting, yet is just minutes away from local restaurants and attractions. The Inn is located in the historic town of Spencertown, New York, just minutes from Chatham, New York’s charming downtown Main Street and a short drive to the Cultural Berkshires of Massachusetts.

businesses.name: The Alander
businesses.description: The Alander is a newly restored, modern, boutique lodge at the foothills of the Berkshires. The accommodations bring together a relaxed cabin aesthetic with modern design and locally sourced amenities; all within a stone’s throw of countless outdoor activities, farm-to-table dining, eclectic shopping, and culture. Enjoy the onsite restaurant: North Star.

businesses.name: The Wick
businesses.description: Contemporary design and welcoming amenities can be found within The Wick, Hudson, a Tribute Portfolio Hotel. This boutique property featuring 55 guest rooms and suites, two meeting spaces, and many on-site amenities including a Lobby Bar which features small plates.

businesses.name: Berkshire Bike & Board
businesses.description: We are passionate about getting people rolling on 2 wheels. Whether it is just cruising around the neighborhood with your kids, mountain biking in the woods, long road bike rides in the summer or commuting to work, we are dedicated to making sure your ride is as smooth as can be.

businesses.name: Mills Park
businesses.description: A one-acre managed ecological park located approximately .2 miles from the historic center of the Village of Kinderhook.

businesses.name: Mud Creek Environmental Learning Center
businesses.description: Mud Creek Environmental Learning Center (ELC) is located on the property of Columbia County Soil & Water Conservation District office.

businesses.name: Roeliff Jansen Park
businesses.description: The Roeliff Jansen Park entrance is located on Route 22, one half-mile south of the Hillsdale light on the east side of the road. The park includes multiple trails, access to the Roeliff Jansen Kill, several picnic areas, a dog run, a farmers market, a summer youth program, and much more.

businesses.name: Stonykill Coffee & Records
businesses.description: A record and coffee shop in the village of Chatham, New York. Owned and run by locals, we bring refined aesthetic funkiness to Chatham's main street. Located on the second floor of 36 Main St. (above the People's Pub) we got coffee, tea, records, books, tapes, and all other types of treasures. Stop in for a cortado or a coldbrew and buy a record, why don't ya?! Stonykill Coffee & Records 36 Main Street, Flr 2 Chatham, NY 12037

businesses.name: Universal Cafe & Bar
businesses.description: The Universal Cafe & Bar, at the Central House, features an eclectic drink and food menu highlighting seasonal ingredients, regional specialties, local distilleries, breweries and cider mills on-tap.

businesses.name: Scali’s Pizza & Pasta
businesses.description: Italian American cuisine, featuring pizza and pasta.

businesses.name: Verdigris Tea and Chocolate Bar
businesses.description: A variety of teas by the cup, pot, or ounce. We also offer an interesting collection of tea wares, kitchen ware, chocolates, and bakery items.

businesses.name: La Bella Restaurant
businesses.description: An Italian restaurant where the true taste of the old country reigns supreme.

businesses.name: Alltown Fresh
businesses.description: Redefining on-the-go food service by blending convenience with a commitment to exceptional, fresh food choices and premium products.

businesses.name: M&M’s Tap and Tavern
businesses.description: Restaurant and tavern serving wood fired pizza and burgers.

businesses.name: Chatham Brewing
businesses.description: Brewed fresh, brewed local, Chatham Brewing has been named Best Craft Brewery in the Hudson Valley multiple times. Their award-winning selection of beer is made with the finest hops and malts available. 14 varieties of unique ales and lagers, such as Czechr’d Past Pilsner and Farmer’s Daughter Rye, are on tap in the tasting room. Specialty brews appear seasonally. Enjoy a “beer flight” along with local food items. Live music on weekends, outdoor seating.

businesses.name: Hudson Brewing Company
businesses.description: Hudson Brewing Company is a Farm Brewery located in the city of Hudson. Our hand-made industrial-style tasting room serves flights, pints, and tastings and offers brewery tours. Along with over 12 award-winning beers on tap, the very popular in-house-made pineapple cider is served. Come play an antique game and hear the lore of Hudson’s past, for which many of the brews are named. Food is available onsite.

businesses.name: Chaseholm Farm Store
businesses.description: A small multi-generational farm crafting many different styles of cheese, mostly in the French tradition; meats from the grazed cows, various sauerkraut, fermented veggies and raw milk.

businesses.name: Germantown Farmers Market
businesses.description: Connect with the individuals and families who are raising wholesome food and creating high quality products here in the Hudson Valley.

businesses.name: Hudson Farmers Market
businesses.description: Local producers only; meat, eggs, plants & flowers, cheese, fruits & vegetables in season, baked goods & prepared foods, wool, wool products and farm-made crafts. EBT/SNAP machine on site. April-November, Saturdays 9am-1pm (rain or shine)

businesses.name: Taconic Ridge Farm
businesses.description: The property consists of a beautiful 1850’s farmhouse and a charming cottage set on 22 rambling acres and surrounded by miles of farmland.

businesses.name: Dos Amigos
businesses.description: Mexican restaurant with indoor and outdoor seating options.

businesses.name: Coyote Flaco
businesses.description: Mexican bar and grill, fajitas, enchiladas, quesadillas, vegetarian plates, wings and more.

businesses.name: Robert’s Mexican Restaurant & Cafe
businesses.description: Authentic Mexican food and so much more!!! Breakfast, Lunch & Dinner.

businesses.name: OK Pantry
businesses.description: A modern-designed space with warm service, handcrafted beverages, REALLY good coffee, fresh pastries, lifestyle goods, grocery beyond.

businesses.name: Noble House
businesses.description: Noble House is a former inn boasting 7 bedrooms, 7.5 bathrooms, 6 fireplaces, and a multitude of spaces for gathering. This 1830 Federal style farmhouse is the perfect destination for your group.

businesses.name: Meisner’s Heritage Farm Market & Eatery
businesses.description: Shop our seasonal produce, locally sourced meats, cheeses, flowers, provisions, and fresh-baked goods. Enjoy a curated Friday Night Dinner or Weekend Picnic basket featuring everything you’ll need for a memorable (and easy) meal.

businesses.name: Magdalena’s Restaurant
businesses.description: Fresh and authentic Mexican food served for Lunch & Dinner. Vegan, Vegetarian & Gluten Free options can be enjoyed with beer, wine or a cold Margarita! Eat in or take out.

businesses.name: Chatham Flowers and Gifts
businesses.description: An authentic flower shop filled with fresh cut flowers, flowering and green plants, and unique gift items.

businesses.name: The Tin Barn
businesses.description: A revitalized historic Tin Barn in Clermont presented by Dreamaker Collective!

businesses.name: Parker House 1893
businesses.description: The Parker House 1893 in Craryville, NY, offers a refined dining experience that seamlessly blends historic charm with contemporary culinary artistry. Housed in a meticulously restored 1865 Victorian residence, the restaurant provides an intimate setting perfect for special occasions or a sophisticated evening out. Guests are treated to a menu that emphasizes seasonal, locally sourced ingredients, ensuring each dish is both fresh and flavorful. The ambiance is enhanced by elegant décor, attentive service, and a commitment to excellence that caters to the discerning palate. Whether you’re seeking a memorable meal or a venue for a private event, The Parker House delivers an exceptional dining experience in the heart of the Hudson Valley.

businesses.name: Isola Wine & Tapas
businesses.description: A downtown Kinderhook oasis that specializes in wine and tapas from European islands and coasts.

businesses.name: barn & bike
businesses.description: The barn, built in 1930, was once home to livestock but had not been truly used or maintained for decades. Deciding to save the structure, we started by replacing the siding and the roof. Slowly but surely, a vision to create a unique and – yes, spectacular – space took root. With some trepidation we embarked on a two-year renovation journey, transforming the barn into a living space.

businesses.name: Park Theater Hudson
businesses.description: A lovingly restored historic theater hosting live events whose primary mission is to integrate our community through music and art.

businesses.name: The Blue Spruce Inn & Suites
businesses.description: The Blue Spruce Inn & Suites welcomes business travelers and families with a safe, quiet space to work and play. Our newly renovated motel is located on ten acres of park-like grounds in Kinderhook, NY. We are committed to providing you with a clean and comfortable stay and feature premium mattresses, bedding and toiletries. All rooms have a mini-fridge, microwave, coffee maker and flat-screen satellite tv. All suites have a large living room and a separate bedroom, full eat-in kitchen and private bath. Amenities include remote-controlled widescreen television with free wi-fi and cable (complimentary HBO and Direct TV including more than 40 channels), air conditioning, adjustable thermostat, black-out curtains, cots for children, an iron and ironing board, toiletries and a blow dryer. The new, light-filled business center/meeting space includes free high speed wireless internet, a gadget charging station, and ample wall outlets.

businesses.name: Hudson Valley Indoor Golf
businesses.description: Indoor golf facility featuring simulator play, driving range, launch monitor practice, lessons, club fitting, golf club sales and repairs.

businesses.name: Ancram Little Store
businesses.description: Beautifully restored country store serving breakfast and lunch. Fresh baked goods, daily soup special, and deli counter. Don’t forget to check out our selection of items from specialty vendors such as Bread Alone Bakery, Jane’s Ice Cream, and Ronnybrook Farm Dairy.

businesses.name: Blue Moon Chocolate & Gifts
businesses.description: A starlit collection of confections and handmade and delightful gifts. The chocolates, candies & fudge come from the best makers in the cosmos!

businesses.name: Mark’s Pizza
businesses.description: Pizza, wings, calzones, hot subs, and salads.

businesses.name: The Stay at Liberty Farms
businesses.description: A boutique glamping site with 10 luxury canvas cabins, surrounding a central lodge with lounge areas, dining spots, communal cooking facilities, and a camp store.

businesses.name: Germantown Farms
businesses.description: We invite you to get lost in the beauty of agriculture and nature.

businesses.name: Hudson Mariner Hotel
businesses.description: The Hudson Mariner is a beautiful and unique property formerly a Captains home when built in 1810. This lovingly cared for 210 year old home has been operating as a B&B for the last many years, but has recently undergone renovation in 2020 by the new owner.

businesses.name: Circle 46 Gallery
businesses.description: Circle 46 Gallery is a contemporary art gallery featuring local emerging and established artists.

businesses.name: Lili and Loo
businesses.description: A combination of the sleek and the rustic, the ancient and the modern, the raw and the refined. The shop is housed in three adjoining 19th century building with two floors and a 2,000 sq. ft. back yard. Lili and Loo has kept the feel of a private home in each of its 15 rooms.

businesses.name: Mi Tiendita Valatie
businesses.description: Mexican grocery store with daily hot specials.

businesses.name: Morabito’s Sub Shop & Deli
businesses.description: Sub Shop Deli with daily specials and homemade soup. Catering available.

businesses.name: Roosters Roadside Creamery & Grill
businesses.description: Soft serve, hard ice cream, milkshakes, and sundaes! Burgers, hot dogs, french fries, pulled pork, mac and cheese & daily specials.

businesses.name: Clermont State Historic Site
businesses.description: Seven generations of Livingston’s lived at Clermont, a riverside mansion with a rich and varied history. Now a museum, it holds a wealth of info on over two centuries of people, from wealthy land-owners to immigrant servants and enslaved African workers.

businesses.name: Cafe Mutton
businesses.description: A small neighborhood restaurant on a sunny corner serving breakfast and lunch. Our food is simple, but we pay very close attention, and we really like meat. But we also like vegetables (so much), and what we really like more than anything is deliciousness. And for the people who come into our space to feel calm and hopeful and glad and beautiful.

businesses.name: Le Perche Hudson
businesses.description: Le Perche bakery and bar is a French restaurant and wood-burning bakery. It is the new, sister restaurant to Swoon Kitchenbar owned by Jeff and Nina Gimmel. The menu created by partner Chef John Carr is an interpretation of French classics and seasonally inspired cuisine utilizing local farm goods. The drink program includes a largely French wine list, progressive cocktails, local cider, beer, kombucha and house-made shrubs and natural sodas. The wood-burning brick oven bakery focuses on naturally fermented breads, classic viennoiserie, pastries and contemporary plated desserts.

businesses.name: Kitty’s Restaurant & Bar
businesses.description: Kitty’s Restaurant and Bar is the Hudson Valley dinner party that everyone is invited to. Guided by the seasonal bounty of our landscape, we elevate comfort classics with a nod to coastal traditions. Drink a glass of wine you’ve never heard of but now can’t live without, and feel your senses heighten with each perfectly layered bite of our vibrant, inventive dishes.”

businesses.name: The Gnome Bistro
businesses.description: Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, Local Beer, and Local People ~ Join us for a meal to remember!

businesses.name: Our Daily Bread Deli
businesses.description: House-cured kosher-style deli meats, authentic Middle Eastern platters & falafel, European style café with fresh baked artisan breads and exceptionally made, local roasted coffee, and craft beers and local cider. Breakfast, brunch, lunch, and catering; outdoor seating in season.

businesses.name: Little Cat Lodge
businesses.description: Welcome to Little Cat Lodge! Our mountain retreat is the perfect destination for travelers seeking a peaceful escape, whether you’re here for a weekend getaway, outdoor adventure, or simply to relax in the serene surroundings and enjoy a wonderful meal……whether it be brunch or dinner!

businesses.name: Sweet PEAs Hillsdale Country Diner
businesses.description: American diner food and homecooked entrees along with authentic tacos and papusas.

businesses.name: Oakdale Park
businesses.description: Oakdale Park is a 14-acre public park in downtown Hudson, NY, a walkable destination for many residents that is free and open to the public.

businesses.name: North Star
businesses.description: An intimate, upscale-casual dining destination offering locally sourced contemporary American cuisine with a full bar featuring carefully curated wines, beers, and spirits.

businesses.name: Marton & Davis
businesses.description: A charming shop in the Village of Chatham.

businesses.name: Columbia Diner
businesses.description: Diner-style operation offering comfort food and a salad bar, plus Tex-Mex selections, and a kids’ menu.

businesses.name: Plaza Diner
businesses.description: Burgers, breakfast, and other diner fare offered in casual surroundings.

businesses.name: DQ Grill & Chill
businesses.description: Soft-serve ice cream & signature shakes top the menu at this classic burger & fries fast-food chain.

businesses.name: El Sabor de Oaxaca
businesses.description: Family-run Authentic Mexican restaurant. Indoor and outdoor seating.

businesses.name: The Pub
businesses.description: Great local beers, wines, and spirits. Affordable food made with local and organic ingredients when available. | Live Local Music in Philmont NY at the heart of Columbia County, NY.

businesses.name: Ancram Center for the Arts
businesses.description: Serving the Hudson Valley, the Berkshires and Litchfield county, Ancram Center for the Arts brings innovative contemporary performances and vital community programs to the region. It comprises two buildings, the historic Ancram Opera House, an intimate performance hall, and a recently acquired neighboring building (The Annex) which will be renovated to provide much-needed housing for artists and interns, as well as creative space for community programs serving Ancram and beyond.

businesses.name: Art Omi
businesses.description: Art Omi is an arts center situated on 120 acres in New York’s Hudson Valley, featuring the Sculpture & Architecture Park, Newmark Gallery, five distinct residency programs, and arts education for all ages.

businesses.name: Artsee
businesses.description: A gallery for artfully created eyewear and for fine Art as well. Just as in the curation of the eyewear ARTSEE carries, the art shows are curated with an equally eclectic eye. Artists who have shown at ARTSEE hail from the four corners of the Earth.

businesses.name: Berkshire Hills Sculpture Garden
businesses.description: Berkshire Hills Sculpture Garden is on 15 hilly acres of farm land including woods, hedge rows and a seasonal stream. As one walks the Garden, there are marvelous views of the Berkshires including Catamount’s ski slopes.

businesses.name: Clarion Concerts in Columbia County
businesses.description: Clarion Concerts is dedicated to bringing world-class, live chamber music to the Hudson Valley area of New York state. Clarion Concerts seeks to expand the chamber music repertoire through the commissioning and performance of new works by contemporary composers.

businesses.name: Concerts in the Village
businesses.description: Exceptional Classical Music Series performed in Kinderhook, New York. Choral and Orchestra directed by David Smith.

businesses.name: Front Room Gallery
businesses.description: Front Room Gallery specializes in contemporary photography, painting, sculpture, installation and limited edition prints. The gallery offers expert art advisory, design, and installation services tailored to both collectors and institutions.

businesses.name: Hoogs and Crawford Glass
businesses.description: Glassblowing studio and gallery featuring the collaborative artwork of Elizabeth Crawford and Nathan Hoogs. Classes are by appointment.

businesses.name: Hudson House
businesses.description: Hudson House offers a space for public art exhibitions, a location for public arts programming and a visiting artist/curator residency program.

businesses.name: LABspace
businesses.description: An experimental art venue with curated art shows, talks, gatherings and more.

businesses.name: Lightforms Art Center
businesses.description: Lightforms is a center for cultural renewal that brings creative artists and their artwork into the public domain in innovative ways that stimulate dialogue around the inner and outer challenges of our time and attempts to serve the spiritual needs of the human being in their daily lives.

businesses.name: Mettabee Farm & Arts
businesses.description: A 165-acre working farm dedicated to community wellness through organic farming and the arts. Seasonal contra dances, concerts, retreats, special events for children and adults, and educational programming and children’s camps.

businesses.name: Peter Jung Fine Art
businesses.description: We specialize in 19th and 20th Century American and European paintings.

businesses.name: PJ Modern Art
businesses.description: Art gallery specializing in Modernist American works.

businesses.name: PS21: Performance Spaces for the 21st Century
businesses.description: “Where the Arts & Nature Thrive.” Modern state-of-the-art facility. Black Box theater in winter/spring expands in June into a partly open pavilion for the summer season. Features music, dance, drama, film, and workshops for kids and adults.

businesses.name: Shakespeare’s Fulcrum
businesses.description: Shakespeare’s Fulcrum is a 2800 sq ft performance space, culture destination, and self-curating art gallery.

businesses.name: Spencertown Academy Arts Center
businesses.description: Founded in 1972, as a cultural center and community resource serving Columbia County and surrounding areas. Housed in a landmark 1847 Greek Revival schoolhouse, the Academy presents a variety of free and low-cost community arts programs and events including concerts, readings, lectures, and art exhibits as well as arts-related workshops and classes for children and adults.

businesses.name: The Ghent Band
businesses.description: Since 1899 The Ghent Band has performed a classic town band repertoire of marches, overtures, show tunes and more.

businesses.name: The School: Jack Shainman Gallery
businesses.description: The focus of the gallery is to exhibit, represent and champion artists from around the world, in particular artists from Africa, East Asia, and North America, by mounting major exhibitions of their work in the gallery. Open Sat 11am-6pm and by appointment.

businesses.name: Turley Gallery
businesses.description: Turley Gallery was founded in 2021 by its Director, Ryan Turley. The gallery is focused on emerging and established artists, specifically those who push the boundaries of their respective mediums and create work that is fresh, relevant and significant to the contemporary art world.

businesses.name: Valatie Community Theatre
businesses.description: An intimate performance space, experience music and theatrical performance up close and personal.

businesses.name: Clermont Vineyards & Winery
businesses.description: Situated on a former dairy farm and in close proximity to Clermont State Historic Site, Clermont Vineyards boasts dramatic panoramic views of the Catskill Mountains. This small, family-run business crafts their wines in the traditional Portuguese way using the grapes from over 4000 vines on 7-acres.

businesses.name: Cooper’s Daughter Spirits at Olde York Farm
businesses.description: Women-owned and family-operated distillery sourcing Hudson Valley ingredients to make seasonal batch spirits. Cooper’s Daughter Spirits is also one of the few distilleries in America coopering barrels on-site by combing old-age techniques with new-world hand tools. Free tours are available of the distillery and cooperage along with tastings.

businesses.name: Harvest Spirits
businesses.description: On site at the Golden Harvest Farm, which grows 15 varieties of apples on two 100-acre orchards. Apples grown, pressed, fermented, distilled, and bottled at the same location. 60 pounds of apples equals one 750 ml bottle of vodka. Harvest Spirits triple distills their signature Core Vodka and produces Cornelius Applejack, Core Gin, John Henry Single Malt Whiskey, Brandy, and specialty Pear Brandy.

businesses.name: Hudson Chatham Winery
businesses.description: The Hudson Chatham Winery is Columbia County’s first winery. We focus on hybrid varieties ideal for growing in Upstate New York. The vineyard has been expanded with approximately 18 different varietals grown. Walk-ins are welcome to join us by the glass or bottle. Guided wine tastings are for reservations only.

businesses.name: Hudson Valley Malt
businesses.description: The craft brewing and distilling movement starts with malted grains. At Hudson Valley Malt, located in the Germantown Beer Farm, malt is created the old-school heritage way, using traditional floor malting techniques. Every day malt is made with fresh grains provided from local farmers for craft brewers and distillers. Tours are available by appointment.

businesses.name: Old Klaverack Brewery & Tasting Room
businesses.description: The OKB is a nano-brewery; a growing segment of the craft beer industry. Batches are brewed on a 1.5 system and include red amber ales, Belgian specialty Klauver Rachen, and American Rye Pale Ale. Brewmaster Erik Bell uses locally grown hops to produce the highest quality beer.

businesses.name: Old Klaverack Hop Farm
businesses.description: The OKB is a nano-brewery; a growing segment of the craft beer industry. Batches are brewed on a 1.5 system and include red amber ales, Belgian specialty Klauver Rachen, and American Rye Pale Ale. Brewmaster Erik Bell uses locally grown hops to produce the highest quality beer.

businesses.name: Reifenberg Brewing
businesses.description: Friendly, local craft brewer creating classic German and other popular styles including IPAs, Porters, Stouts, American ales, etc. Tasting room also has a selection of NYS wine and hard cider, and allows outside food brought in (local restaurant menus available).

businesses.name: Beebe Hill State Forest
businesses.description: 2,018 acres of multiple use area managed for timber production, watershed protection, wildlife habitat, and recreation. Featured activities include hiking, camping, paddling, fishing, hunting and trapping, fire tower, horseback riding.

businesses.name: Borden’s Pond Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: 62 acres, 1.75 miles of trails, easy to moderate hiking

businesses.name: Copake Country Club
businesses.description: 18 hole, Par 72; 6100 yards – Riding cart rentals, pull cart rentals, pro-shop, golf lessons; restaurant; public course – season membership; group rates available; credit cards accepted VS/MC.

businesses.name: Drowned Lands Swamp Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: 114 acres, 1.5 miles of trails, easy to strenuous hiking

businesses.name: Greenport Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: 737 acres, 7.17 miles of trails, easy to moderate hiking

businesses.name: Hand Hollow State Forest
businesses.description: 518 acres of wooded property and some open brush land, with a 10 acre lake on the southern portion of the property called Spiegelberg Lake. Featured activities include hiking, biking, cross-country skiing, snowshoeing, and wildlife observation.

businesses.name: Hand Hollow Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: 447 acres, 2.8 miles of trails, easy to moderate hiking

businesses.name: Harrier Hill Park Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: 6 acres, 1.5 miles of trails, easy to moderate hiking

businesses.name: Harris Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: The Harris property includes 1.9 miles of trails on 76 acres of land. The hemlock forests provide habitat for a variety of different birds. In the temporary vernal pools, you may see the eggs of frogs and salamanders in the spring, and their young as they hatch. These woodland pools provide critical habitat for amphibians. The property’s cliffs and outcroppings are home to lichens over a century old. The small piles of rocks form crevices and caves that provide great habitat for bats, porcupines, and snakes.

businesses.name: Harvey Mountain State Forest
businesses.description: 2,007 acres of multiple use area managed for timber production, watershed protection, wildlife habitat, and recreation. Featured activities include hiking, camping, paddling, fishing, hunting and trapping, fire tower, horseback riding.

businesses.name: High Falls Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: 47 acres, 1.5 miles of trails, easy to moderate hiking

businesses.name: Hudson River Islands State Park
businesses.description: Accessible only by boat, Hudson River Islands State Park facilities are concentrated on the islands of Gay’s Point and Stockport Middle Ground. These islands are fragile communities with many rare and endangered plant and animal species. Day use facilities include picnic areas with grills and nature trail. Fishing is popular and transient camping is allowed.

businesses.name: Lebanon Valley Speedway
businesses.description: DIRT sanctioned stock car racing. April-September; mid-week special events July-August.

businesses.name: Lewis A. Swyer Preserve
businesses.description: 95 acre preserve is one of only five freshwater tidal swamps in New York State. A half-mile boardwalk on the preserve allows visitors to look for migrating waterfowl and shorebirds, breeding birds including green-backed heron, ducks and passerine birds. Bald eagles nest along the river and kingfishers have been spotted on the creek. Beaver and muskrats make their homes along the banks. Fish in the shallow waters include white perch, minnows, killifish, and bluegills.

businesses.name: Lido’s 2 Wildlife Preserve Area
businesses.description: Private and public hunts on 1000 acres devoted to hunting duck, deer, and turkey. Also, members can trout fish and camp out on the club property.

businesses.name: Livingston State Forest
businesses.description: 319 acre Livingston State Forest features scenic views of the Hudson River and opportunities for a variety of uses. There are no designated hiking trails on the property; however, hiking is allowed throughout the forest. This property is managed for multiple uses, including timber production, watershed protection, wildlife habitat and recreation. Featured activities include camping and hunting and trapping.

businesses.name: Martin Van Buren National Historic Site
businesses.description: The home and farm of the founder of the Democratic Party and the eighth president of the United States.

businesses.name: Meadowgreens Golf Course
businesses.description: A 9 hole, Par 36; 3012 yards golf course – Pull cart and riding cart rentals, public course.

businesses.name: New Forge State Forest
businesses.description: 612 acre forest features a stream-side canoe launch on Taghkanic Creek. The property is managed for multiple uses, including timber production, watershed protection, wildlife habitat, and recreation. Featured activities include camping, paddling, fishing, and hunting and trapping.

businesses.name: New York Golf Park
businesses.description: New York Golf Park is a PGA recognized facility just minutes from Hudson, situated in a idyllic countryside setting to enjoy the outdoors and a variety of fun golf related activities for the entire family. NYGP features a Driving Range, Miniature Golf Course and other facilities on the property.

businesses.name: Ooms Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: 180 acres, 2.9 miles of trails, easy to moderate hiking

businesses.name: Rogers Island Wildlife Management Area
businesses.description: This Hudson River island consists of 281 acres of forested upland, tidal forested wetlands, tidal marshes, and extensive mudflats. Acquired in 1950 to provide refuge to migrating waterfowl, this Wildlife Management Area can only be accessed by boat since there is no legal public access across the railroad tracks that border the east side of the property. The best place to land a boat is at the southwest corner of the island just north of the Rip Van Winkle Bridge. The various upland and wetland habitat types found on the WMA support a great variety of fish, wildlife, and plant species, including several rare plants. Rogers Island contains one of the largest tidal swamp forests in the Hudson Estuary and exemplary examples of freshwater tidal marsh and intertidal mudflat communities.

businesses.name: Schodack Island State Park
businesses.description: 1,052 acres on the eastern shore of the Hudson River. The park has been designated a State Estuary, and a portion of the park shelters a Bird Conservation Area that is home to bald eagles, cerulean warblers, and herons that nest in the cottonwood trees. Eight miles of multi-use trails wind through a variety of ecological communities. In addition, the park has 66 campsites for use, an improved bike trail, volleyball nets, horseshoe and a kayak/canoe launch site. Interpretive signage highlights the park’s historic and environmental significance.

businesses.name: Schor Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: 233 acres, 2.5 miles of trails, moderate to strenuous hiking

businesses.name: Siegel-Kline Kill Public Conservation Area
businesses.description: 55 acres, 1.5 miles of trails, easy to moderate hiking

businesses.name: Spot Light Theater
businesses.description: We are dedicated to providing quality family entertainment and exceptional value. We have eight all digital projection and sound screens. We offer gift passes, birthday parties, group showings, and more please contact us for more information.

businesses.name: Stockport Flats
businesses.description: 5 mile narrow mosaic of landforms, including from north to south Nutten Hook, a bedrock outcropping; Gay’s Point and Stockport Middle Ground Island, dredge features that are both part of the Hudson River Islands State Park; the mouth of Stockport Creek, a large tributary stream; a portion of the upland bluff south of Stockport Creek; the dredge spoils and tidal wetlands between Stockport Creek and Priming Hook; and the northern end of Priming Hook. The Hudson is entirely tidal freshwater at this site. Featured activities include boating the marshes, picnic, observe wildlife, and hike the trails.

businesses.name: Stockport Wildlife Management Area
businesses.description: 357 acres of fields, forested uplands, tidal forested wetlands, tidal marsh, and small areas of freshwater wetlands. Featured activities are hiking, hunting and trapping, fishing, and wildlife.

businesses.name: Stonykill Disc Golf Course
businesses.description: 18 hole disc golf course, free and open to the public year round.

businesses.name: Wilson M. Powell Wildlife Sanctuary
businesses.description: A great deal of natural diversity in approximately 145 acres including a stream, a marshy area, glades and Reilly Pond. The Sanctuary provides habitat for Barred Owls, Common Ravens, Veery, Louisiana Waterthrushes and many other song birds. There is a small marshy pond and fern-caparisoned stream valley, and the cliff face itself holds treasures of ferns and lichens not found elsewhere in the Sanctuary. Dorson’s Rock, overlooking the magnificent Hudson Valley offers opportunities for hawk watching and unusual flora. Do take a short hike up to Dorson’s Rock for panoramic views across the Hudson Valley to the distant Catskill Mountains, scanning the sky for Red-shouldered, Red-tailed and Broad-winged Hawks.

businesses.name: Winding Brook Country Club

businesses.name: Filli’s Deli and Bakery
